text.title: SOC2 Control-to-Repo Artifact Map Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of soc2-control-to-repo-artifact-map enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Every SOC2 control listed in the audit scope is mapped to at least one live repo artifact (config, runbook, IaC file) — not just policy documents.
rule.rationale: Policies without operating evidence fail SOC2 Type II.

rule.statement: The map is committed in the repo (YAML/Markdown) — not exclusively in a GRC tool.
rule.rationale: Repo is the source-of-truth engineers actually maintain; GRC tools drift behind reality.

rule.statement: Each control entry names: artifact path, owning team, evidence retention SLA, last-verified date.
rule.rationale: Without these, the map is unusable at audit time.

rule.statement: Audit log evidence is retained per the strictest applicable SLA (typically ≥12 months for Type II).

rationale.source: AICPA SOC2 Type II evidence requirements

rule.rationale: Short-retention logs invalidate the year-long audit window.

rule.statement: Every control is re-verified at most quarterly; stale verification triggers a remediation work item.
rule.rationale: Audit periods are 6-12 months; quarterly verification keeps drift bounded.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, skip this methodology and route to the adjacent one referenced in Related.
rule.rationale: A skip rule keeps the decision tree closed: every branch ends at a real rule id.
